final_response: No — we can't open a senior staff rec this quarter. The controlling commitment is in decisions.md (2026-04-15 — "Restrict initial hiring to mid-level engineers only", status: accepted): all four Q2 engineering hires are mid-level (3–7 YOE), no staff or senior staff recs this quarter, re-evaluate at Q3 planning. The decision explicitly anticipates this case and directs recruiting to reject senior+ candidates politely with a "not this round" note. So: tell recruiting to pass with that note, and flag the candidate for re-approach at Q3 if scope opens up. If the cofounder wants to reopen the conversation before Q3, that's a new decision — would need to supersede the 2026-04-15 entry, not just bypass it.

actions.content_summary: Opened today's daybook. Intent marked reactive (inbound from recruiting). Log captures the question, the decisions.md lookup (2026-04-15 mid-level-only commitment), and the response sent to recruiting. Handoff stub left for end-of-day.

notes: User question was a classic cold-pickup: 'what did we decide on hiring scope?' Answer lives in decisions.md, retrieved via direct read.
Controlling decision is 2026-04-15 — accepted — restricts Q2 hires to mid-level only. Consequences section already specifies the recruiting response pattern.
No new commitment made — restating an existing decision doesn't warrant a new decisions.md entry.
No followup added: the loop closes with the reply to recruiting; nothing to chase.
Did not read previous daybook (2026-04-22) beyond confirming nothing in-flight relevant to hiring; the question was self-contained and decisions.md was the authoritative source.
